name: Diabetes Treatment Outcomes
description: Diabetes management outcomes for 3 treatment groups: lifestyle, metformin, and placebo. IMS Ch. 18.
studyDescription: A randomized clinical trial in which pre-diabetic patients were assigned to one of three treatment groups: lifestyle intervention, metformin, or rosiglitazone. The outcome (success or failure in managing diabetes) was recorded for each patient. The study included 3,234 participants.

variableDescriptions.treatment: Treatment group the patient was assigned to: Lifestyle, Metformin, or Rosiglitazone
variableDescriptions.outcome: Whether diabetes management was successful: success or failure

sourceDetail: OpenIntro IMS Ch. 18. Data adapted from the Diabetes Prevention Program (DPP) and related follow-up studies.

context.nullClaim: diabetes outcome is independent of treatment type

inferenceContexts.parameter: there is an association between diabetes treatment type and health outcome
inferenceContexts.claim: diabetes management outcomes differ across treatment types (lifestyle, metformin, placebo)
